acceptance_targets: [ch03_booker_001] Pass if a reader can compute the same remaining balance from the stated total (445) using only revised lines 121-143, 201-209, and 313 with no double-counting or implied off-page transfers; fail if two different balances are still plausible.

[ch03_booker_002] Pass if revised lines 263-317 contain one explicit admission naming the transfer (what he did and amount) and one irreversible communication action (a sent message or spoken disclosure) before chapter close; fail if the chapter still closes on withheld truth only.

[ch03_craft_001] Pass if revised lines 17 and 289-299 contain at least one unambiguous flip-phone detail and no smartphone-only descriptors; fail if the object can still be read as a touchscreen slab phone.

[ch03_craft_002] Pass if revised lines 267-273 include Eli stating the transfer plainly (including amount) and Cora responding with a concrete boundary, demand, or refusal; fail if the passage resolves into abstract labels without explicit admission.

[ch03_craft_003] Pass if revised line 9 includes explicit quoted speech for Eli's reaction and that speech directly precedes or motivates his pull-over action; fail if the beat remains summarized as a generic swearing tag.

[ch03_dialogue_001] Pass if each targeted speaker uses contractions in at least 3 of 5 eligible finite clauses within those spans, with any remaining full forms clearly functioning as emphasis or formality; fail if uncontracted phrasing remains the default texture.

[ch03_dialogue_002] Pass if anonymizing speaker tags in those revised spans still lets a reader attribute each line to the correct character by diction and rhythm alone in most cases; fail if lines remain easily interchangeable across the three voices.

[ch03_prose_001] Pass if the revised 129-319 span avoids repeating the same 'like + noun phrase' sentence pattern more than twice while preserving concrete sensory specificity; fail if the pattern still recurs three or more times in that span.

[ch03_prose_002] Pass if revised lines 269-272 contain no standalone noun-phrase fragment sentences and still bridge cleanly into Eli's defensive line at 273; fail if clipped nominal fragments remain.

[ch03_prose_003] Pass if only one of the three targeted spans states Eli's disclosure strategy explicitly and the other two convey it through action/dialogue behavior; fail if all three still explain the pattern overtly.

[FBR-003] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:877 to snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:880 contains one clear suppression beat (not two stacked variants) and still lands Eli's avoidance with concrete stakes. Fail if the line remains a repeated 'He almost... He almost...' chain.

[FBR-007] Pass if chapter_03 contains only this one retained 'landed like' construction and it remains anchored to scene-specific stakes. Fail if additional formula-metaphor echoes are introduced in the same chapter.

[FBR-013] Pass if Eli's line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:839 sounds colloquial and pressure-reactive (with contractions) while preserving the same meaning. Fail if it remains formal/uncontracted legal-sounding speech.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can compute the same remaining balance from the stated total (445) using only revised lines 121-143, 201-209, and 313 with no double-counting or implied off-page transfers; fail if two different balances are still plausible.
findings.problem: The money choreography for tow balance, deposit, shift cash, and remaining repair balance does not reconcile cleanly on-page, which weakens causal trust at the chapter's core logistical conflict.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise transaction logic around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:313 so one explicit ledger governs all exchanges (who holds each amount, when Darlene starts work, and what balance remains).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised lines 263-317 contain one explicit admission naming the transfer (what he did and amount) and one irreversible communication action (a sent message or spoken disclosure) before chapter close; fail if the chapter still closes on withheld truth only.
findings.problem: The chapter's confession arc peaks at an almost-admission and then ends on another unsent message, so the scene-level state change is muted instead of decisively escalating consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen end-state around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:305, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:317 by requiring one irreversible action in-scene (explicit admission to Cora or a sent message that names the offense).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised lines 17 and 289-299 contain at least one unambiguous flip-phone detail and no smartphone-only descriptors; fail if the object can still be read as a touchscreen slab phone.
findings.problem: Continuity contradiction: the continuity sheet canonizes Eli's device as a prepaid flip phone, but this chapter describes a burner with a peeling plastic screen protector in a way that reads as touchscreen-phone coding.
findings.rewrite_direction: Align device description in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:289, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:291, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:299 with flip-phone physical behavior (hinge, keypad, small screens) or remove touchscreen-specific cues.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised lines 267-273 include Eli stating the transfer plainly (including amount) and Cora responding with a concrete boundary, demand, or refusal; fail if the passage resolves into abstract labels without explicit admission.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's moral pressure point: the prose names the theft context, then retreats into abstraction ('Partial confession. Partial debt.') instead of rendering accountability in-scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:273, replace abstract gloss with concrete admission language (actor, amount, victim, intent) and an immediate relational consequence from Cora.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised line 9 includes explicit quoted speech for Eli's reaction and that speech directly precedes or motivates his pull-over action; fail if the beat remains summarized as a generic swearing tag.
findings.problem: Timidity in high-pressure rendering: 'He swore' summarizes panic-language instead of presenting the utterance, softening impact in the first mechanical failure beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:9 to include Eli's actual spoken outburst (register-appropriate profanity or verbal reaction) tied to his immediate decision to signal and coast.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each targeted speaker uses contractions in at least 3 of 5 eligible finite clauses within those spans, with any remaining full forms clearly functioning as emphasis or formality; fail if uncontracted phrasing remains the default texture.
findings.problem: Dialogue register drifts stiff through repeated uncontracted forms ('Do not,' 'You are,' 'That is,' 'I did not') across high-pressure contemporary speech, conflicting with style-bible default high contraction use.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust spoken lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:275 so default speech contracts unless deliberate emphasis is clearly motivated.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if anonymizing speaker tags in those revised spans still lets a reader attribute each line to the correct character by diction and rhythm alone in most cases; fail if lines remain easily interchangeable across the three voices.
findings.problem: Darlene, Vera, and Cora intermittently converge into the same clipped admonition cadence, reducing idiolect separation in multi-speaker shop/diner sequences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate speaker textures around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:227: keep Darlene mechanically diagnostic, Vera process-and-tempo managerial, Cora relational and socially surgical.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised 129-319 span avoids repeating the same 'like + noun phrase' sentence pattern more than twice while preserving concrete sensory specificity; fail if the pattern still recurs three or more times in that span.
findings.problem: Recurring comparison template ('... like ...') appears repeatedly beyond twice, creating a noticeable formula that flattens otherwise strong sensory prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework concentration points in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:215, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:285, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:319 by mixing direct concrete description and varied figurative structures rather than repeated 'like' similes.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised lines 269-272 contain no standalone noun-phrase fragment sentences and still bridge cleanly into Eli's defensive line at 273; fail if clipped nominal fragments remain.
findings.problem: Narrative clipping in 'Partial confession. Partial debt.' reads as telegraphic fragmenting, conflicting with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:269 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271 into full grammatical prose that preserves the beat but avoids fragment shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if only one of the three targeted spans states Eli's disclosure strategy explicitly and the other two convey it through action/dialogue behavior; fail if all three still explain the pattern overtly.
findings.problem: Eli's stress profile ('logistics first, controlled disclosure') is explained directly multiple times, which reads as over-literal reuse of style-bible character notes rather than emergent characterization.

Fail if the line remains a repeated 'He almost... He almost...' chain.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Eli's line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:839 sounds colloquial and pressure-reactive (with contractions) while preserving the same meaning. Fail if it remains formal/uncontracted legal-sounding speech.
findings.problem: Dialogue register mismatch against style-bible contraction guidance (Eli = high contraction). In a casual diner exchange, Eli defaults to formal uncontracted diction ('I did not...'), and this drift recurs in later chapters.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Eli's quoted line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:839 using natural spoken contraction and defensive rhythm consistent with his high-contraction profile under stress.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
